steps.title: Identify what both texts agree on
steps.content: Both texts discuss Impressionist painters making deliberate choices about frames. Text 1 says Degas "insisted" on specific frames and Impressionists "chose" colorful frames to distinguish themselves. Text 2 says frames were chosen "for aesthetic reasons" to "harmonize" with paintings. The point of agreement is that Impressionist painters were intentional about frame selection — even if the texts disagree on the motive.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Gold frames were considered especially desirable by those who purchased works from Impressionist painters.: Neither text says buyers wanted gold frames; in fact both texts imply buyers of Impressionist works received painted frames. ✗
  • Choice B — The colors in an Impressionist painting were often chosen to complement the colors of the frame it would be placed in.: This reverses the relationship: Text 2 says frame colors were chosen to match the painting, not the other way around. ✗
  • Choice C — Many Impressionist painters were intentional about the frames they selected for their works.: Both texts confirm this — Text 1 through Degas's insistence and the movement's deliberate choice of colorful frames, Text 2 through the aesthetic reasoning behind frame color selection. ✓
  • Choice D — Degas's preferred framing style was different from that of most Impressionist painters.: Text 1 explicitly presents Degas as representative of Impressionist practice, not an exception to it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
